name: loves-executioner
description: Irvin Yalom's Love's Executioner — psychotherapy and existential casebook exploring the four existential givens (death, freedom, isolation, meaninglessness) through ten unforgettable patient stories and the therapist's own humanity
useCase: Understand existential psychotherapy, explore the four givens of existence, learn from ten therapy case studies, and apply existential wisdom to your own life
knownRisks: Clinical stories are disguised and condensed for narrative effect — not all therapy looks like this
Yalom's existential approach is one school among many in psychotherapy
Self-diagnosis based on case studies can be misleading
